findings.description: self.hit_history.append(hit) is inside the loop 'for expert_name in EXPERTS' (7 iterations). hit_history gets 7 entries per prediction instead of 1. This corrupts ALL downstream metrics: mean_hit_rate (line 263), rolling_30_hit (line 262), max_drawdown (line 268), stability_score (line 274), and total_predictions (line 331) reports 7x actual count. FIX: move hit_history.append() outside the loop, or track it once per prediction.

self.transition = TransitionController() is created at line 52 but transition.update() is never called anywhere in the codebase. The transition mechanism for smoothing state changes is completely non-functional. The component occupies memory and has methods but zero effect on the system.

findings.issue: ExpertManager hardcodes only 5 experts, missing AntiTrend and Cycle
findings.description: get_expert_weights() iterates only [Gap, Trend, Momentum, Frequency, Color], but MetaStrategyV2.EXPERTS has 7 experts including AntiTrend and Cycle. Same issue at lines 137 and 147. AntiTrend and Cycle are never tracked for freeze/recovery, their hit rates are never computed, and their freeze reports are never generated. FIX: change hardcoded lists to use EXPERTS constant.

findings.issue: Duplicate Jaccard penalty - applied twice with same threshold/factor
findings.description: Jaccard similarity penalty is applied TWICE: (1) ExpertDiversityPenalty.apply_penalty() at meta_strategy_v2.py:201 with threshold=0.8, factor=0.7, then (2) ExpertAggregator._apply_diversity_penalty() at line 124 with the SAME threshold=0.8, factor=0.7. This double-penalization likely over-weakens correlated expert pairs. The two components should have differentiated responsibilities or one should be removed.
StateRouter.apply_color_weight() is never called from any code path. Color activation is handled by ColorActivator.inject_color() instead. The method in StateRouter is defined but unreachable.

findings.issue: calc_expert_correlation is dead code - never called
findings.description: ExpertAggregator.calc_expert_correlation() is defined but never called from any production code path. It's a diagnostic method that returns pairwise expert correlations. Marked as '用于诊断' but no caller exists.
StateDetector.get_scores() is never called anywhere in the codebase. It returns raw vote counts which are not directly meaningful. The detect_state() method handles state detection internally.

findings.issue: Dead pass statement with explanatory comment
findings.description: The line 'pass  # 状态切换由detector的三期确认机制处理' is a no-op comment indicating state transition is handled by the detector. There's no actual code here - it's just a comment explaining why no code exists. Either implement something or remove the comment.

findings.issue: Inconsistent expert list in get_all_reports - only 4 experts
findings.description: get_all_reports() only iterates [Gap, Trend, Momentum, Color], missing Frequency, AntiTrend, and Cycle. This is inconsistent with get_expert_weights() and get_recent_hit_rates() which both include Frequency.

findings.issue: Conditional report fields cause inconsistent last_report structure
findings.description: last_report['penalties'] is only added when sim > similarity_threshold. last_report['exploration'] is only added under specific entropy/random conditions. Consumers of agg_report cannot rely on these fields being present. Consider always including these keys with empty values.

duplicate_logic.details: Both ExpertAggregator._apply_diversity_penalty() and ExpertDiversityPenalty.apply_penalty() compute Jaccard(top6_a, top6_b) with threshold=0.8 and penalty_factor=0.7. Applied sequentially in predict_top6() at lines 201 then 51-54. Double application with identical parameters likely over-penalizes.

ExpertAggregator._apply_forced_exploration() and ChaosHandler.get_explore_probability()/get_replacement() both implement exploration logic for high-entropy scenarios. ExpertAggregator uses random replacement with diversity_scores weighting; ChaosHandler uses dominance_counter and separate exploration. Both are invoked in predict_top6() flow.

state_leaks.issue: _current_expert_scores stored as instance attribute then cleared
state_leaks.details: prediction_history.append(self._current_expert_scores) stores a reference to the expert_scores dict, then _current_expert_scores is set to None. If the dict is modified later (e.g., by normalization in aggregate()), the stored history reflects those modifications. Should store a deep copy.

summary: Code audit found 10 findings across 6 critical components. CRITICAL bugs: (1) hit_history gets 7x entries per prediction due to loop-append bug, corrupting all metrics; (2) TransitionController is dead - instantiated but never updated; (3) ExpertManager only tracks 5 of 7 experts, leaving AntiTrend/Cycle unmanaged. MEDIUM issues: (4) Jaccard penalty applied twice (ExpertAggregator + ExpertDiversityPenalty) with identical parameters; (5) apply_color_weight() is dead code. LOW issues include additional dead code (calc_expert_correlation, get_scores, pass statement) and inconsistent report structures. The hit_history bug is the most urgent - all backtest metrics are unreliable until fixed.
